methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Baykar is a private-stage defense and security company founded by Özdemir Bayraktar, currently assigned a rumored valuation near $10B. The broader defense and security sector comprises 24 peers across public and private markets, including 2 publicly listed comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers such as Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

The peer-median methodology is not applicable to this analysis. The defense and security universe lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage private comparables at similar valuation tiers—zero datapoints met the filtering criteria requiring a minimum of three peers. Expanding to an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable distortion given Baykar's specialized position and the heterogeneity of defense subsectors. The available public comparables (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS) operate at substantially different scales and geographies, limiting their direct relevance.

Without a robust triangulation framework, establishing a defensible valuation range is premature. The $10B rumored valuation cannot be validated against standard multiples or peer benchmarks given data constraints. Key uncertainties include Baykar's revenue scale and margin profile relative to its public and private peer set, the company's stage of commercialization, and capital structure. Further disclosure of financial performance and comparable transaction data is required to support quantitative valuation work.
